evidence.support: DermaMNIST leakage detection and partition correction workflow.
HAM10000 duplicate detection and human-confirmation workflow.
Corrected/extended dataset generation and benchmark workflow.
Fitzpatrick17k duplicate and cluster analysis.
Label correction, duplicate removal, and standardized split generation.

value: Join DermaMNIST image filenames to HAM10000 lesion metadata.
Detect lesion identity appearing in multiple partitions.
Move overlapping lesion images to a single partition to create DermaMNIST-C.
Construct DermaMNIST-E using external ISIC validation and test partitions.
Compute image embeddings and pairwise cosine similarities using fastdup.
Manually inspect high-similarity pairs and clusters.
Remove duplicates and erroneous images and standardize partitions to create Fitzpatrick17k-C.
Repeat classification benchmarks on original and corrected datasets.

evidence.support: The abstract states the audit, correction, and reproducibility contributions.
The concluding discussion restates the corrected and extended datasets.
The article provides Zenodo datasets and a public code repository.

value: Systematic quality audit of DermaMNIST, HAM10000, and Fitzpatrick17k
Identification of duplication, leakage, mislabeling, and image-processing flaws
Release of DermaMNIST-C, DermaMNIST-E, and Fitzpatrick17k-C
Public release of analysis code and corrected datasets

calibration_design.review_notes: No optical, camera, colorimetric, or spectral calibration procedure is defined.

camera_assumptions.review_notes: No camera spectral-sensitivity, radiometric-response, exposure, or device-calibration model is provided.

capture_protocol.review_notes: The source audits previously acquired images and does not prescribe a new capture protocol.

chromophore_representation.review_notes: Melanin, hemoglobin, and other chromophores are not modeled or estimated.

evidence.support: Lesion identifiers are used to detect and correct cross-partition leakage.
fastdup embedding similarity and manual review are used to classify duplicate and nonduplicate pairs.
The Fitzpatrick17k analysis uses similarity thresholds and clustering to identify duplicate pairs and groups.
ResNet-18 and ResNet-50 benchmarks are repeated on original, corrected, and extended datasets.

audit_components: Metadata-based lesion and image identity analysis
Cosine-similarity ranking followed by manual visual confirmation
Duplicate clustering using a similarity threshold

evidence.support: The three audited datasets are named and their analysis scope is stated.
HAM10000 is described as containing 10,015 dermoscopic images.
The source summarizes original and corrected dataset versions, including Fitzpatrick17k's 16,577 images.

audited.name: DermaMNIST
audited.role: Derived benchmark dataset audited for leakage, resizing, and benchmark validity

audited.name: HAM10000
audited.role: Source dataset for DermaMNIST and lesion-identity analysis

audited.name: Fitzpatrick17k
audited.role: Clinical dermatology dataset audited for duplication, diagnosis labels, skin-type labels, and partitions

evidence.support: The source defines pairwise embedding-similarity analysis and manual duplicate confirmation.
The Fitzpatrick17k analysis applies similarity thresholds and cluster criteria.

equations.review_notes: The packet preserves the article's rendered mathematical notation imperfectly; the operational similarity relationship and threshold are nevertheless explicit.
value: Duplicate-pair or duplicate-cluster threshold based on similarity at or above approximately 0.90

evidence.support: Partition leakage is quantified by images and lesions.
The benchmark reports AUC and overall accuracy.
Duplicate analysis reports similarity, pairs, and clusters.

illumination_assumptions.review_notes: Illumination variation appears as a possible source of visual duplication or segmentation difficulty, but no illuminant model or controlled-light assumption is defined.

instrument_or_camera.review_notes: The study analyzes existing image datasets and does not specify a canonical acquisition instrument or camera system.

evidence.support: DermaMNIST is traced to HAM10000 and its image-resolution transformation.
Image-pair similarity is compared with lesion-identity metadata.
Fitzpatrick17k image and label content is examined for duplication and mislabeling.

value: Dermoscopic images and metadata from HAM10000 and DermaMNIST
Clinical dermatology images and labels from Fitzpatrick17k

evidence.support: The study reports image and lesion overlap across dataset partitions.
Cosine similarity and manually confirmed duplicate classes are observed.
AUC and accuracy are the reported benchmark metrics.
The source reports diagnosis-label, skin-type-label, duplicate, and split corrections.

physical_model.review_notes: The study is a dataset-quality and benchmark audit. The source does not define a light-tissue physical model.

evidence.support: The abstract identifies duplicates, partition leakage, mislabeling, and test-partition problems as the study target.
The study scope explicitly covers DermaMNIST, HAM10000, and Fitzpatrick17k and reports correction of identified defects.

problem_addressed.value: Quality defects in widely used dermatological image datasets, including duplicate images, train-validation-test leakage, mislabeled images, metadata errors, inappropriate image resizing, and absent or unsuitable held-out test partitions.

evidence.support: The source documents threshold-bound duplicate detection and manual-review limitations.
The discussion identifies benchmark noncomparability, threshold misses, manual-review limits, and segmentation challenges.

value: Similarity thresholds may miss true duplicates whose scores fall below the selected cutoff.
The scale of manual review prevents a guarantee that Fitzpatrick17k-C is entirely free of duplicates.
Automated healthy-skin segmentation is sensitive to illumination, image quality, multiple objects, and diffuse pathology boundaries.
DermaMNIST and DermaMNIST-C benchmark values should not be directly compared because their training and evaluation partitions differ.

evidence.support: The source reports exact image and lesion overlap counts across DermaMNIST partitions.
The source reports AUC and accuracy for original, corrected, and extended DermaMNIST versions.
The source summarizes corrected dataset sizes, split composition, and the fraction of DermaMNIST lesions affected by leakage.

value.benchmark_observation: Corrected and extended datasets produce materially different AUC and accuracy profiles; DermaMNIST-E is more challenging than the original benchmark.

evidence.support: HAM10000 includes multiple images of lesions acquired at two clinical sites.
Leakage is quantified using images and unique lesion identifiers.
Fitzpatrick17k duplicate analysis is performed at image-pair and cluster levels.

sample_or_subject_population.value: The unit of analysis is the image, lesion identity, image pair, or duplicate cluster. The article does not establish a unified subject-level cohort across the three datasets.

scattering_representation.review_notes: Optical scattering is not modeled or estimated.

evidence.support: The authors state that they uncover quality issues, measure benchmark effects, propose corrections, and release the analysis pipeline.
The closing interpretation restates the corrected and extended datasets and the objective of exposing hidden data-quality defects.

scientific_objective.value: Systematically audit DermaMNIST, its source dataset HAM10000, and Fitzpatrick17k; quantify the effects of identified quality defects; and release corrected datasets and reproducible analysis code.

evidence.support: DermaMNIST and HAM10000 are image and lesion-label datasets.
Fitzpatrick17k includes clinical images, diagnosis labels, and Fitzpatrick skin-type labels.

skin_representation.value: Skin is represented through existing dermoscopic or clinical RGB images, disease labels, lesion identities, and Fitzpatrick skin-type labels. No continuous spectral skin representation is introduced.

evidence.support: The paper explicitly links dataset quality defects with benchmark reliability.
Partition leakage is shown to threaten benchmark validity.
The discussion details data, split, label, and evaluation limitations.
Public data and code support a traceable evidence chain.

value.not_established: The paper does not establish a physics-based skin-color decomposition, spectral reflectance recovery method, camera observability model, capture control boundary, or conditioned skin spectral representation.
value.relevance: The study establishes that duplicate identity, partition leakage, label integrity, image-processing provenance, and explicit train-validation-test construction materially affect reported dermatology-model performance. It supports TrueColor's requirement to separate data-quality and evidence-chain failures from model or physical-model performance.

evidence.support: Candidate duplicates are verified through manual review.
Benchmark experiments are repeated three times and reported as mean plus or minus standard deviation.

value.benchmark_validation: ResNet-18 and ResNet-50 classification experiments repeated three times
value.duplicate_validation: Embedding-similarity detection followed by manual visual confirmation
value.reported_summary: Mean and standard deviation across repeated benchmark runs

title: Investigating the Quality of DermaMNIST and Fitzpatrick17k Dermatological Image Datasets.
